ncs_standard_definition.description_short: An Architectural Journalist writes engaging articles and stories about buildings, architecture, and the built environment for various publications.
ncs_standard_definition.description_detailed: They conduct research, interviews, and site visits to gather information, and then craft compelling narratives that inform and entertain readers about architectural trends, designs, and innovations.

first_stepper.description: High school students interested in architecture, writing, or journalism can pursue a degree in a related field.

choice_maker.description: Those with a generic degree can take courses or certifications in architecture, writing, or journalism to bridge the gap.

future_builder.description: Experienced journalists or writers looking to specialize in architecture can take advanced courses or certifications.
future_builder.prerequisite_experience: 2+ years of experience as a journalist or writer

problem_solver_uturn.description: Individuals from unrelated fields can transition into architectural journalism by developing relevant skills and building a portfolio.
portfolio_proof_required: Published articles or blog posts on architecture

roadmap.title: Capstone & Portfolio
topics: Build a portfolio of published articles or blog posts
